channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.nintendolife.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.nintendolife.com

item.title: Capcom Celebrates Resident Evil 30th Anniversary With Multiple Announcements
item.content:encoded:
New theme park collabs, RE2 Arcade and more

As part of its Spotlight showcase today, Capcom took the time to acknowledge the 30th anniversary of Resident Evil. This special occasion will officially take place on 22nd March 2026, and to kick things off, it's today shared multiple announcements.

First up was the reveal of a new Resident Evil Requiem collaboration with Universal Studios Japan. This will immerse fans in the world of the game like "never before" and will be a large-scale project due out in 2026, with more updates to come.

A new arcade light gun Bandai Namco unit based on Resident Evil 2 has also been announced. It's officially titled Resident Evil 2: Arcade and is equipped with "air jets and floor vibration technology" so players can "feel the fear" in every part of their body as they shoot zombies.

And to add to this, Capcom will also be hosting 30th anniversary known as "Symphony of Legacy". Tickets for the World Tour are now on sale, with more details available on the official website.

Last but not least, was a reminder about the Resident Evil Generation Pack, which is available until 31st March 2026.

What do you think of these 30th anniversary announcements for Resident Evil? Anything else you would like to see? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.title: Video: Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection Launch Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Arriving on Switch 2 next week

Ahead of the launch of Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection on the Switch 2 next week, Capcom has released one final trailer. It's also got an anime trailer on the way.

The latest game trailer shows Azuria and Vermeil locked in conflict, as the two countries and twin Rathalos engage in battle.

Capcom has also revealed details about save data unlocks for the existing titles Monster Hunter Stories, Monster Hunter Stories 2 and Monster Hunter Rise. It will give players special unlockable armour and more. There's also pre-order bonuses in the form of additional armour.

Once again, there's a demo for Twisted Reflection available on the Switch eShop right now and the game arrives on 13th March 2026.

We'll update this post with more details soon.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Capcom's New Game Pragmata Is Now Releasing A Little Earlier Than Expected
item.content:encoded:
Same month, new date

During today's Capcom Showcase, it was revealed the upcoming puzzle-shooter Pragmata would be arriving a little earlier than expected.

Instead of 24th April 2026, it's now arriving on 17th April 2026. Capcom has also shared a look at a new feature known as Shelter in a new trailer and gameplay footage. The Diana amiibo will also be releasing soon.

When the official trailer for this announcement is shared, we'll add it to this story. There's also a demo you can play of Pragmata right now on the Switch 2.

Will you be picking up this one on release? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.
